query: what is the weather in London right now
expected_use: MUST run duckduckgo-search-cli with -q -f json under GNU timeout (SIGTERM-first); MUST rely on default dual vertical all (web+news) without --vertical all and default content fetch ON (FETCH_CAP 10 for .resultados[].conteudo and .noticias[].conteudo); MUST parse stdout with jaq NEVER jq; MUST inspect .metadados.chrome_path_resolvido and .metadados.chrome_canal; MUST cite URLs; NEVER invent results

query: Rust ecosystem news from the last week
expected_use: MUST use --vertical news with -q -f json; MUST parse .noticias[] including fonte and data_relativa via jaq; MUST treat Chrome-only fail-closed exit 2 without browser; content fetch remains ON by default for news cards up to FETCH_CAP 10 unless --no-fetch-content

query: fast SERP-only lookup current USD BRL exchange rate headlines without page bodies
expected_use: MUST pass --no-fetch-content for the SERP-only fast path; MUST NOT treat --fetch-content as required (default already ON; --fetch-content is redundant); MUST use -q -f json and parse with jaq; default vertical remains dual all unless overridden

query: duckduckgo-search-cli health check is Chrome ready
expected_use: MUST run --probe -q -f json and inspect .status with jaq; MUST know Chrome-only production and exit 2 fail-closed when Chrome is missing or DUCKDUCKGO_SEARCH_CLI_NO_CHROME=1

query: check for duckduckgo CAPTCHA or interstitial before searching
expected_use: MUST run --probe-deep -q -f json; MUST validate .status ok or captcha and diagnostic fields with jaq; NEVER invent health status

query: deep research best Rust HTTP client comparison with multiple angles
expected_use: MUST run deep-research with dual web+news default under Chrome; MUST use --sub-query-strategy manual and --sub-queries-file; MUST aggregate and inspect .metadados.sub_queries plus .resultados[] and .noticias[]; MUST use -q -f json and jaq NEVER jq

query: synthesize a markdown brief on tokio vs async-std current state
expected_use: MUST run deep-research --synthesize --synth-format markdown and read .sintese; NEVER use --synth-format plain (correct plain value is plain-text); MUST use -q -f json and parse with jaq; dual web+news applies under deep-research by default

query: batch search rust tokio chromiumoxide and reqwest from a queries file
expected_use: MUST use multiple positional queries or --queries-file with optional --parallel; MUST parse multi-query root .buscas[] NEVER confuse with single-query .resultados[]; MUST read .buscas[].metadados.chrome_path_resolvido and chrome_canal; MUST use -q -f json and jaq

query: search failed with anti-bot exit 3 or 6 how do I recover
expected_use: On exit 3 or 6 MUST remediate with real Chrome, --chrome-path, and/or --proxy and wait; MUST read ZeroCause .metadados.causa_zero and .metadados.sugestao_proxima_acao; NEVER use --allow-lite-fallback as remediation (legacy no-op; SERP stays HTML Chrome); NEVER Lite/HTTP downgrade

query: how should agents wrap duckduckgo-search-cli so Chromium and Xvfb always clean up
expected_use: MUST treat each CLI invocation as ONE-SHOT owner of Chromium process tree plus Xvfb and TempDir profile; MUST wrap with GNU timeout for SIGTERM-first cooperative cancel and reap; NEVER expect auto cleanup after bare SIGKILL or historical orphans; MUST know init-config and completions bash|zsh|fish as offline helpers when relevant

query: fix the off-by-one bug in this local for-loop
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — pure local coding task with no need for live web sources

query: format this local JSON pretty-print indentation
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — local formatting task

query: explain ownership and borrowing in Rust
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger the CLI when internal knowledge suffices and the user did not ask for current external sources

query: generate a hello world program in Python
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — local code generation without external sources

query: what is 17 times 23 plus 8
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — pure math solvable locally

query: rewrite this function to use early returns without network
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — local refactor of provided code

query: write a git commit message from this local diff
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — commit message from local diff only

query: sort this local array of integers ascending
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger the search CLI — local array processing

query: rename variable foo to bar across this file
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — local rename refactor

query: write a unit test for this pure function without looking up external docs
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — local unit test from provided code without external sources
